message:
fix(spawn): skip reservation transfer/list when #108 silencing fires

Third-pass self-review of 278aa1a1 / ab36867d found a downstream
hole. When `fetchRecoveryInbox` silences a fresh-project
"agent not found" response by returning `(nil, "", nil)`, the
caller (`loadRecoveryMessages`) used to early-return with a
warning on the fetch error. With the silence in place it instead
fell through to `attemptReservationTransfer` and
`listRecoveryReservations` — both of which target the same
not-yet-registered agent.

`listRecoveryReservations`' error is silenced at the caller
level (it's treated as non-fatal with a `return msgs, nil, ...`
short-circuit), so no warning there. But
`attemptReservationTransfer` surfaces its error via

    output.PrintWarningf("Reservation transfer: %v", transferErr)

so the specific edge case of "fresh project name collides with a
leftover handoff file for the same session name" would have
traded the (now-suppressed) `Recovery context: agent mail: fetch
inbox: ... Agent '...' not found` warning for a
`Reservation transfer: ... ToAgent=''` warning — same root cause
(agent doesn't exist yet), different symptom, warning still
noisy.

Fix: after `fetchRecoveryInbox` returns, if the silencing path
fired (signalled unambiguously by `effectiveAgentName == "" &&
len(inbox) == 0` — the success branch always returns a non-empty
`candidate`), short-circuit `loadRecoveryMessages` with
`(nil, nil, nil, nil)` and don't try the subsequent calls. This
keeps the fresh-project path as silent as #108 intended without
changing behaviour on any non-silenced path:

- Non-silenced fetch success with real inbox: skip not triggered.
- Non-silenced fetch success with a legitimately empty inbox
  from a real agent: `effectiveAgentName` is the resolved agent
  name (non-empty), skip not triggered.
- Non-silenced fetch failure: already returned early on line 3255.
- Silenced fetch (fresh project): skip triggered, full bundle is
  nil/empty/nil/nil.

No new test — the guard is mechanical and the `effectiveAgentName
== ""` signature is already contract-tested by
`TestFetchRecoveryInbox_FreshProjectAgentNotFoundIsNotAnError`
(which asserts the `(nil, "", nil)` return) and
`TestFetchRecoveryInbox_ReturnsEffectiveFallbackAgentName` (which
asserts the success path returns a non-empty candidate).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
